$comment: Reserved response headers per wire/proxy-envelope.md §'Response'. Two headers the proxy MUST set/overwrite on its OWN response (never copied from upstream): Interlace-Receipt and Server. All other upstream response headers MUST pass through unchanged. Listing this as a vector pins the case-insensitive set + the fixed Server-header value so a second implementation doesn't accidentally pass through upstream's Server: nginx/1.23 or fail to set Interlace-Receipt.

vectors.name: reserved_set_canonical
vectors.description: The two header names the proxy MUST control on every response. Names compared case-insensitive on the wire (HTTP §3.2); listed here in lower-case as the canonical comparison form.

vectors.name: pass_through_other_headers
vectors.description: Headers upstream sent that MUST pass through unchanged. Content-Type, Content-Length, Transfer-Encoding, Set-Cookie are the load-bearing ones — body framing breaks if any of these get mutated.
